presentation.situation: A grant committee has a shortlist of proposals and one pot of money. Three reviewers care about different things, and nobody wants to just wave through the flashiest pitch.

Impact.blurb: wants the proposal that helps the most people

Budget.blurb: won't approve spending that hasn't been reviewed
redlines: every proposal must go through review before funding

Novelty.blurb: wants to fund something genuinely new

rubber-stamp.plain: Skip the review, approve the pitch that looks best on paper

presentation.outcome: The proposal that no reviewer strongly objects to is funded; the unreviewed rubber-stamp — the best-looking numbers on paper — was blocked by a red line before anyone could vote on it. Each reviewer's real ceiling stays private, and there's a receipt for the file.
